name: ColliderUtil
description: The `ColliderUtil` library handles registration of collider components with the physics system.

content.text: The `ColliderUtil` library handles registration of collider components with the physics system. Colliders in Universe must be explicitly registered from their `OnInit` method using one of these calls before they participate in collision detection or trigger events. For trigger detection (entering, exiting, staying), use the `@`-field pattern on the host script rather than these registration methods directly.
Register a collider component with the physics system. Call from `OnInit` of the collider script.
Registers the calling script as a box (rectangular cuboid) collider. The shape is defined by the `size` property on the script.
Registers the calling script as a sphere collider. The shape is defined by the `radius` property on the script.
Registers the calling script as a capsule collider. Capsules are commonly used for characters.
Registers the calling script as a mesh collider, using the host object's mesh for precise collision. More expensive than primitive colliders.
Registers the calling script as a terrain collider — used specifically with terrain objects.
Registers the calling script as a trigger sphere. Trigger colliders detect overlap but do not physically block movement.
Collider registration must happen in `OnInit`. Calling a registration method after init has no effect.
Mesh colliders are significantly more expensive than box, sphere, or capsule colliders. Reserve them for static geometry only.
Trigger colliders do not block movement — they only fire enter/exit events. Use the `@OnTriggerEnter` magic method on the host script to respond.
For character collision, prefer `AddCapsuleCollider` — capsules handle step-climbing and slope-sliding better than boxes or spheres.

lines: // BoxCollider OnInit — register this script as a box collider
// SphereCollider OnInit — register as a sphere trigger
// On the object that owns the collider, receive trigger events
